---
title: "Methods"
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## Learning Goals: The Toolbox
1. Be able to point to the following brain regions on a picture: *left hemisphere*, *right hemisphere*, *frontal lobe*, *temporal lobe*, *parietal lobe*, *occipital lobe*
1. Be able to "navigate" to a region of the brain using the following terms: *anterior*, *posterior*, *inferior*, *superior*, *medial*, *ventral*, *sulcus*, *gyrus*
1. Identify and distinguish *coronal*, *axial*, and *sagittal* views of the human brain
1. Understand the difference between spatial and temporal resolution
1. Know the names of the major research tools that are used to study language in the brain, what is measured by each tool, and the major strengths and weaknesses of each method
1. Understand how scientific papers are meant to be read
:::

| Date | Topic | Reading | Resources
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Thurs 9/3 | Methods | SGN Ch. 2: *The Toolbox*  |  [<span class="blue-badge">Slides ↗</span>](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-DlaIOIvnQBYJNo8rJH51RvTGDmv6JFJAnk2MSbpzUQ/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p)[<span class="purple-badge">Poll ↗</span>](https://www.menti.com/blzw41uh6xjx)
| Tues 9/8 | *No class (Monday schedule)* | | | 
| Thurs 9/10 | Interpreting brain data | McCabe & Castel 2008 | | | 


<!-- Mentimeter
intro: temp check

1. Label the lobes
label, one at a time:
* lobes
* anterior, posterior, inferior, superior, medial, ventral

how much did you understand of today's class?
muddiest point?
-->
